summary: This notebook demonstrates how to run inference using the VGGT (Visual Geometry-Grounded Transformer) model on a sequence of images. It loads a pretrained model, processes input images, and extracts camera poses, depth maps, and 3D point clouds. The code also includes a visualization pipeline using Rerun.io to render the reconstructed geometry and camera trajectories in 3D space. Readers learn how to integrate a modern vision transformer for structure-from-motion tasks and apply confidence-based filtering to visualize only high-quality 3D points. The notebook serves as a practical starting point for exploring VGGT's capabilities in image matching and 3D reconstruction.
competition_flow: Loads a sequence of input images, passes them through a pretrained VGGT model to extract camera poses, depth maps, and 3D point clouds, then visualizes the reconstructed geometry and camera trajectories using Rerun.io.
data_reading: Uses glob to collect image paths from the competition directory
Passes paths to vggt.utils.load_fn.load_and_preprocess_images for tensor conversion
data_processing: Automatic mixed precision (bfloat16 or float16) during inference based on GPU capability
Percentile-based confidence thresholding (90th percentile) to mask low-confidence depth and point values
insights: VGGT can directly output camera poses, depth maps, and 3D point maps from image sequences without traditional SfM pipelines.
The model provides explicit confidence scores for both depth and points, enabling effective filtering of noisy geometry.
Rerun.io provides an efficient way to visualize 3D reconstruction results directly in a notebook environment via .rrd file export.
